title: Namespace tools — source, branch, and workflow
purpose: Implement the remaining namespace tools beyond the critical messaging and member tools. These cover development operations (source control, branch management) and workflow dispatch with scheduler commands absorbed from the former admin tool. Together with NR-006, they complete the Meridian tool surface. The former admin tool was dissolved: workspace/profile ops → meridian_member, channel/search ops → meridian_messaging, scheduler → meridian_workflow. Project tool deferred. Command surfaces aligned with TA-006, TA-007, TA-008.
task: Implement three namespace tools in crates/meridian-tools/src/: SourceTool (meridian_source, 25 commands), BranchTool (meridian_branch, 22 commands), WorkflowTool (meridian_workflow, 14 commands including scheduler commands absorbed from former admin). Each follows the namespace pattern from NR-006 with command discriminant. Register all tools via register_meridian_tools with profile gating.

requirements.title: SourceTool — meridian_source namespace
requirements.spec: SourceTool SHALL implement norn::tool::Tool. The command enum and per-command parameter structs SHALL use #[derive(ToolArgs)] from norn-macros to generate input_schema. The tool SHALL use tool_follow_ups! to register output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md. 25 commands: status, file_statuses, branches, log, blame, diff_file, diff_summary, tree_show, tree_add, tree_remove, tree_merge, tree_restack, tree_graft, worktree_list, worktree_create, worktree_remove, stage, unstage, stage_all, discard, commit, push, pull, fetch, remotes. Source operations SHALL execute against the workspace working directory resolved from CallerContext.workspace_id. When the agent session is focused on a shared workspace, the working directory resolves to the virtiofs mount path for that workspace (see DESIGN.md D13 workspace focus). Aligned with TA-006.
acceptance: SourceTool implements Tool with name() returning 'meridian_source'
Command enum and per-command parameter structs use #[derive(ToolArgs)]
tool_follow_ups! macro registers output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md
input_schema includes command enum with all 25 commands
status command returns working tree state
file_statuses command returns per-file change state
branches command lists branches
log command returns commit history with optional limit and ref spec
blame command returns line attribution for a file
diff_file command returns diff for a specific file
diff_summary command returns summary diff statistics
tree_show command displays the branch tree structure
tree_add command adds a branch to the tree
tree_remove command removes a branch from the tree
tree_merge command merges within the tree
tree_restack command rebases the tree
tree_graft command grafts branches in the tree
worktree_list command lists worktrees
worktree_create command creates a worktree
worktree_remove command removes a worktree
stage command stages specific file changes
unstage command unstages specific file changes
stage_all command stages all changes
discard command discards uncommitted changes
commit command commits staged changes with a message
push command pushes to remote
pull command pulls from remote
fetch command fetches from remote without merging
remotes command lists configured remotes
All results are structured JSON, not raw shell output
Service errors (git failure, workspace not found, etc.) return ToolOutput with is_error=true and human-readable error message

requirements.title: BranchTool — meridian_branch namespace
requirements.spec: BranchTool SHALL implement norn::tool::Tool. The command enum and per-command parameter structs SHALL use #[derive(ToolArgs)] from norn-macros to generate input_schema. The tool SHALL use tool_follow_ups! to register output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md. 22 commands spanning branch lifecycle, stack operations, and PR management: branch_status, branch_submit, branch_land, branch_transition, branch_list, branch_show, branch_advance, stack_status, stack_submit, stack_sync, stack_land, stack_restack, pr_status, pr_create, pr_push, pr_sync, pr_approve, pr_request_changes, pr_comment, pr_merge, pr_close, pr_request_reviewers. Branch operations SHALL call the source control service layer for branch stack management. Aligned with TA-007.
acceptance: BranchTool implements Tool with name() returning 'meridian_branch'
Command enum and per-command parameter structs use #[derive(ToolArgs)]
tool_follow_ups! macro registers output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md
input_schema includes command enum with all 22 commands
branch_status command returns branch lifecycle state
branch_submit command submits a single branch for review
branch_land command lands a single branch
branch_transition command changes the current branch
branch_list command lists branches with metadata
branch_show command shows branch details
branch_advance command advances a branch
stack_status command returns the full stack tree state
stack_submit command submits an entire stack
stack_sync command syncs the stack with main
stack_land command lands an entire stack
stack_restack command rebases the stack
pr_status command returns PR state
pr_create command creates a PR for a branch
pr_push command pushes to the PR branch
pr_sync command syncs a PR with main
pr_approve command approves a PR
pr_request_changes command requests changes on a PR
pr_comment command adds a comment to a PR
pr_merge command merges a PR
pr_close command closes a PR
pr_request_reviewers command requests reviewers for a PR
Service errors return ToolOutput with is_error=true and human-readable error message

requirements.title: WorkflowTool — meridian_workflow namespace (with scheduler commands)
requirements.spec: WorkflowTool SHALL implement norn::tool::Tool. The command enum and per-command parameter structs SHALL use #[derive(ToolArgs)] from norn-macros to generate input_schema. The tool SHALL use tool_follow_ups! to register output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md. 14 commands spanning workflow dispatch and scheduler management: workflow_list, workflow_show, workflow_run, workflow_status, workflow_history, workflow_cancel, scheduler_list, scheduler_create, scheduler_get, scheduler_update, scheduler_delete, scheduler_enable, scheduler_disable, scheduler_run. The scheduler commands are redistributed from the former meridian_admin tool. Workflow operations SHALL call the workflow service layer. Aligned with TA-008.
acceptance: WorkflowTool implements Tool with name() returning 'meridian_workflow'
Command enum and per-command parameter structs use #[derive(ToolArgs)]
tool_follow_ups! macro registers output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md
input_schema includes command enum with all 14 commands
workflow_list command lists available workflows
workflow_show command shows workflow details and parameters
workflow_run command dispatches a workflow by name with parameters
workflow_status command returns execution status by ID
workflow_history command returns execution history
workflow_cancel command cancels a running execution
scheduler_list command lists configured scheduled tasks
scheduler_create command creates a new cron-scheduled task
scheduler_get command returns scheduler details by ID
scheduler_update command modifies a scheduler's configuration
scheduler_delete command removes a scheduler
scheduler_enable command enables a disabled scheduler
scheduler_disable command disables an active scheduler
scheduler_run command manually triggers a scheduled task
Scheduler commands scope to CallerContext.workspace_id
Service errors return ToolOutput with is_error=true and human-readable error message

dependency_setup: No new cargo dependencies. All required crates are established by NR-006's meridian-tools crate setup. The meridian-tools crate already depends on norn, norn-macros, serde, serde_json, thiserror, tracing, uuid, and tokio.
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement project namespace tool (deferred — not set up yet)
SHALL NOT create a meridian_admin tool — former admin actions are redistributed to existing namespace tools per C65
SHALL NOT modify the messaging or member tools (NR-006 scope — admin actions redistributed there are NR-006 updates)
SHALL NOT bypass CallerContext authorization in any tool
SHALL NOT modify the norn crate
verification: All 3 namespace tools have valid input_schema with command enum
SourceTool has 25 commands, BranchTool has 22 commands, WorkflowTool has 14 commands
Each tool's execute method returns structured JSON for all commands
No meridian_admin or meridian_project tool exists
